chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَجُوزُ مِنَ الْوَقْفِ وَ الصَّدَقَةِ وَ النُّحْلِ وَ الْهِبَةِ وَ السُّكْنَى وَ الْعُمْرَى وَ الرُّقْبَى وَ مَا لَا يَجُوزُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ عَلَى الْوَلَدِ وَ غَيْرِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 23 – What is allowed from the endowment, and the charity, and the grant, and the gift, and the (provision) of habitation, and the lifetime (service) and the bondman, and what is allowed from that upon the child and others

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/52)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/52)
text: الرَّجُلِ يَجْعَلُ لِوُلْدِهِ شَيْئاً وَ هُمْ صِغَارٌ ثُمَّ يَبْدُو لَهُ أَنْ يَجْعَلَ مَعَهُمْ غَيْرَهُمْ مِنْ وُلْدِهِ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazal Bin Shazaan, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Abdul Rahman Bin Al Hajaaj,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the man who made something to be for his children and they were small. Then it seemed to him that he should name, along with them, others from his children (as sharers). He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No problem’.
en.sarwar: 9. Muhammad ibn ’Isma‘il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from ibn abu ‘Umayr from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj who has narrated the following: “About the case of a man who gives something to his small children then he decides to make his other children share it with them, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is not harmful.’”
